claim: The shift in coding from directly manipulating text in an IDE to having models write code via agents represents the most significant change in AI coding over the past year.
actionable: Adopt agent-based workflows in Claude Code or similar tools instead of relying solely on traditional text-based IDE editing.
source_title: The future of agentic coding with Claude Code

claim: A quality harness (system prompt, context management, tools, MCP servers, settings, permissions) around the model matters more than prompt quality alone for agentic coding performance.
actionable: Invest in robust harness engineering and infrastructure around your LLM rather than solely focusing on prompt optimization.
source_title: The future of agentic coding with Claude Code

claim: The best evaluation method for coding agents is real-world usage ('vibes check') rather than synthetic benchmarks, because the breadth of tasks is too wide to capture with fixed evals.
actionable: Conduct internal dogfooding of your coding agent with real work tasks before relying heavily on standardized benchmarks for quality assessment.
source_title: The future of agentic coding with Claude Code

claim: CLAUDE.md files in code repositories provide an extensibility point for agents to understand project context without modifying the harness.
actionable: Create CLAUDE.md files in project roots documenting architecture, naming conventions, and project-specific guidance for Claude Code to reference.
source_title: The future of agentic coding with Claude Code

claim: For beginners using Claude Code, the first workflow should be asking it to explore and explain the codebase before asking it to write code.
actionable: Start new Claude Code sessions by querying the agent about codebase structure (e.g., 'How do I add a new logger?') before requesting code generation.
source_title: The future of agentic coding with Claude Code

claim: Claude Code tasks should be categorized by difficulty (easy, medium, hard) to choose the right interaction pattern: direct GitHub PR for easy tasks, plan mode for medium, paired coding for hard.
actionable: Classify your coding task by scope/difficulty before invoking Claude Code; use /plan for medium tasks and manual pairing for hard ones.
source_title: The future of agentic coding with Claude Code
